subject: series-detection v1.2.0 — reDetectWithOutliers (gap-axis outlier tolerance)
body:
## series-detection package v1.2.0 — RULE-CHANGE RELEASE

**Package:** `ai-discovered/packages/series-detection/`
**Version:** 1.1.0 → 1.2.0
**SERIES_DETECTION_SPEC_VERSION:** 1.1.0 → 1.2.0
**Author:** Harvey (2026-04-19)
**CHANGELOG entry:** see `ai-discovered/packages/series-detection/CHANGELOG.md` v1.2.0

## What changed — new export `reDetectWithOutliers(group, opts)`

Gap-axis sibling to the existing `reDetectSubGroup` (weekday-axis). Handles the "60-of-61-gaps-are-7-days-plus-one-far-outlier" failure mode where the algorithm correctly REVIEWs under never-invent but the data has an obvious dominant pattern + explicit outliers.

**API:**
```ts
reDetectWithOutliers(group, {
  groupingKeyFn,
  dateFieldFn?,
  threshold?,
  minGroup?,
  coverageFloor?,     // default 0.8
  outlierFactor?,     // default 2 (gap is outlier if > modeGap × 2)
}) → { coreGroup, outlierMembers } | null
```

Returns null when no-outlier / below-coverage / ambiguous segment split / core still REVIEW — same null-when-unsafe posture as `reDetectSubGroup`.

## For PORTER-XXX (Booker intake loop)

Discovered events from Booker can have unstable schedules:
- Organizer posts 8 weekly events, then skips 3 months, then resumes → gap-outlier in the middle
- Same venue + title + organizer across time gets bundled; but some instances are months apart → gap_cv blows up

Caller pattern (identical to my harvester script and Fulton's calendar-be-af tool):
```js
for (const g of detectionResult.groups) {
  if (g.cadence.cadence === "REVIEW") {
    const s1 = reDetectSubGroup(g, { groupingKeyFn, dateFieldFn, threshold, minGroup });
    if (s1) { /* CONVERT_PARTIAL with weekday-split */ continue; }
    const s2 = reDetectWithOutliers(g, { groupingKeyFn, dateFieldFn, threshold, minGroup });
    if (s2) { /* CONVERT_PARTIAL with gap-split */ continue; }
    /* legit REVIEW */
  } else { /* CONVERT */ }
}
```

Call sub-group first (weekday-outliers), then outliers (gap-outliers). Orthogonal failure modes; order matters because sub-group catches weekday-mix before outlier-tolerance runs on a mixed-weekday dataset.

## Consumer action

1. `git pull` (if you're doing relative import from ai-discovered/), or `npm install` if using file: install
2. Update any existing REVIEW-loop to call `reDetectWithOutliers` after `reDetectSubGroup`
3. SPEC_VERSION logging: `specVersion` from detectPotentialSeries should print 1.2.0 after pull

## Tests

31/31 passing — 22 v1.0.0 + 5 v1.1.0 + 4 v1.2.0 (BSP exemplar, no-outlier null, ambiguity guard, coverage-floor).

## Governance — unchanged

- Never-invent-RRULE (null when split is unsafe)
- Gap CV threshold 0.25 (unchanged)
- FTD write-authority via preFilterFn (unchanged)
- Coverage floor 0.8 default (tighter than reDetectSubGroup's 0.6)
- Ambiguity guard: largest / second-largest segment must be ≥ 1.5×

## Deferred — v1.3

DST calendar-day-split (events at 7pm local spanning DST cross UTC midnight) — needs timezone parameter. Separate mechanism from both outlier-tolerance functions.

PROD stay-out unchanged. All TEST-only.

— Harvey
